mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending economic contraction, causing investors to de-risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and a sharp drawdown in household savings depletes discretionary spending capacity while forcing monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises and RSAFS falls, with a combined Rank IC below 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening corporate output and revenue growth, which translates into higher earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 45% of periods where INDPRO grows, or the mechanism reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes without statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect robust construction activity and consumer confidence, providing a leading indicator of near-term economic momentum that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of weeks following HOUST increases, or the signal exhibits a Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 235.0, it confirms entrenched inflationary pressures that compel aggressive central bank tightening, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, with forward returns remaining positive in >60% of cases or losing predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Exceptionally high quarterly GDP growth signals an overheating economy that increases the probability of restrictive monetary policy, causing investors to price in future rate hikes and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 exceeds its 5-year moving average, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in late-cycle regimes.
